title: /who
text: /who Display the information about the Uniface configuration. /who { = } { 1 } | 2 Parameters 1 —display basic information about the Uniface installation configuration, including release or update, directories, $RSO and $RES resources paths, character sets, and assignment files used. If a UAR is referenced in $RES and it contains a version record, the version information is also displayed, and if the UAR is not available at the expected location, this is also reported. 2 —list the installed drivers and paths, as well as the information displayed by 1. Use With ide.exe —Uniface IDE uniface —Uniface Runtime urm —Resource Manager Description Display the Uniface defaults determined at installation time, as well as the paths established by the global assignment file USYSADM:usys.asn and the local assignment file. When a deployment archive is referenced in the [RESOURCES] section, and it has a version record ( uar:ver/name.ver ), the contents of that record is also shown. Example: /who=2 for default Uniface Installation The following shows the output for /who from the basic Uniface Installation: UNIFACE 10.4 .03 ---------------------------------------------------------------------- Executable = C:\Program Files\Uniface\Uniface 10 10.4.03\common\bin\ide.exe Application = Development Environment Release update = 10.4.03.024 (024 0904_1) - September 04, 2025 Patch = 024 Platform: Operating system = MSW: Windows 11 (W11) usysbin directory = C:\Program Files\Uniface\Uniface 10 10.4.03\common\bin\ usysadm directory = C:\Program Files\Uniface\Uniface 10 10.4.03\uniface\adm\ usys directory = C:\Program Files\Uniface\Uniface 10 10.4.03\common\usys\ working directory = C:\Users\UserName\Uniface 104 Enterprise Edition\project\ $RSO = .\resources $RES = C:\Program Files\Uniface\Uniface 10 10.4.03\common\usys\ide.uar Archive (ide) created with : 10.4.03.024 (024 0902_1) Archive (ide) creation date : 03 Sep 2025 16:12:52 Archive (ide) description : Uniface IDE Archive (ide) version : 20250903_1556_b435ec06 $RES = C:\Program Files\Uniface\Uniface 10 10.4.03\common\usys\ide_messages.uar Archive (ide_messages) created with : 10.4.03.024 (024 0902_1) Archive (ide_messages) creation date : 03 Sep 2025 16:12:59 Archive (ide_messages) description : Uniface IDE messages Archive (ide_messages) version : 20250903_1556_b435ec06 $RES = C:\Program Files\Uniface\Uniface 10 10.4.03\common\usys\ide_classic.uar Archive (ide_classic) created with : 10.4.03.024 (024 0902_1) Archive (ide_classic) creation date : 03 Sep 2025 16:07:39 Archive (ide_classic) description : Uniface IDE Hybrid Archive (ide_classic) version : 20250903_1556_b435ec06 $RES = C:\Program Files\Uniface\Uniface 10 10.4.03\common\usys\usys.uar Archive (usys) created with : 10.4.03.024 (024 0902_1) Archive (usys) creation date : 03 Sep 2025 16:13:03 Archive (usys) description : Uniface USYS Archive (usys) version : 20250903_1556_b435ec06 Assignment file = C:\Program Files\Uniface\Uniface 10 10.4.03\uniface\adm\usys.asn Assignment file = C:\Program Files\Uniface\Uniface 10 10.4.03\uniface\adm\ide.asn $SYS_charset = CP1252 $DEF_charset = CP1252 $WEB_charset = CP1252 Copy This list shows default selected drivers, and drivers recognized by the kernel, so when support was dropped for customers, they are still visible here. Selected drivers = PSV(1.0) XML MSW(1.0) UD0 UD1 UD2 Selected drivers = UD3 UD4 UD5 UD6 UD7 UD8 UD9 TCP Selected drivers = TLS SLE SNA --- Paths --- $SLE=$INS_SLE $MSW=$INS_MSW $PSV=$INS_PSV $TCP=$INS_TCP $SNA=$INS_SNA $XML=$INS_XML $UD0=$INS_UD0 $UD1=$INS_UD1 $UD2=$INS_UD2 $UD3=$INS_UD3 $UD4=$INS_UD4 $UD5=$INS_UD5 $UD6=$INS_UD6 $UD7=$INS_UD7 $UD8=$INS_UD8 $UD9=$INS_UD9 $TLS=$INS_TLS $CHR=$INS_CHR $MW1=$INS_MW1 $COM=$INS_COM $JAV=$INS_JAV $TUX=$INS_TUX $CIC=$INS_CIC $IMD=$INS_IMD $IDF=$INS_SLE $DEF=$INS_SLE $SYS=$INS_SLE $UUU=$INS_SLE $GUI=$INS_MSW $DBMS=$INS_SLE $DBMS_DEF=$INS_SLE © 1986-2025 Rocket Software, Inc. or its affiliates.
